response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries from the bushes. These blueberries were the start of their collection. David gathered more than Emily and his amount was related to Sophia's. His count was 15 more than Emily's and also three times what Sophia had. This created a chain of numbers that connected their efforts.

***

David collected 39 blueberries because he had 15 more than Emily. This number was also three times what Sophia had. To find out how many Sophia collected, divide David's total by three. Sophia had 13 blueberries. The total blueberries collected by all three were 24 plus 39 plus 13. This added up to 76 blueberries. They had a lot of blueberries to work with.

***

They used the blueberries to make muffins. Each batch required 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make, divide the total blueberries by 9. This gave them 8 full batches with some blueberries left over. Each batch was sold for $5. Multiplying the number of batches by the price per batch gave the total money made. The total was $40.

***

The money they made from selling the muffins was $40. This was the result of their hard work and the blueberries they collected. Their efforts brought in a good amount of money. The youth showed their ability to work together and make something valuable from nature's gifts.
